coverage.name: Bloom Energy
coverage.thesis: **Our one live catalyst long, held into the QQQ-vs-$718 fork.** Live 3 sh @ $222.64 (a late/top-tick entry, ~-3% underwater), paper 40 sh @ $218.90; $188 GTC stop confirmed on each. The catalyst is real and specific: a $1.7B Oaktree/IDF financing to deploy Bloom fuel cells for Nebius' AI data-center power — an energy-for-AI theme decoupled from the chip-capex fear crushing the rest of tech, which is exactly why BE was the watchlist's best long Friday (+4.0%) on a red tape. But it's a battleground: it faded from a $223.6 spike to $214.5 and a 'priced-for-perfection' short case is live. Q2 earnings 7/28 is the next real catalyst. A catalyst swing on a wide stop, not a trend — hold, don't add into a risk-off open.
coverage.hold_reason: We own BE as the clearest catalyst name on the list - a $1.7B Oaktree/Nebius deal to power AI data centers, running into 7/28 earnings, and it decoupled from the chip selloff last week. Our live entry was late (top-tick $222.64) so we've caught little of the move, but the multi-day swing thesis is intact and the $188 stop is wide below structure. Pre-market it's ~flat with no fresh catalyst; what we're watching: if the open confirms a green tape we'd let it lift, and only add the dry $201 on real strength (not the open print) - a failure back below ~$205 lets the $188 stop work.
coverage.size_note: Own both books; $188 stop each. Add only on confirmation, not the open print.

projection.basis: faded from $219 open; catalyst to 7/28 but red today

coverage.name: ProShares UltraPro Short QQQ
coverage.thesis: **The paper book's hedge and the reason it can own the green on a down day.** 210 sh @ $42.54, $38 GTC stop. With QQQ ~3.2% under its 20-day and a fresh oil/Iran risk-off flare, a 3x-inverse-Nasdaq sleeve is the offset that pays while the longs bleed — it was +4.6% Friday, one of only two green watchlist names. We hold it as insurance, not a naked short: it partially offsets the paper NVDA/PLTR/RKLB net-long chip drag. Live can't cleanly carry it (PDT + wash constraints), so this capture lives in paper. On a confirmed $718 reclaim on volume, this is the first thing we cover/trim to free cash for the strongest long.

It's the piece that lets paper actually own a green name on a risk-off day instead of sitting fully offside. What we're watching: a QQQ reclaim of its $718 20-day on real volume is the signal to cover/trim it and rotate that cash into the strongest confirmed long — until then the hedge stays on.
